chapter_summary: On the first day of the week, women find the tomb empty and angels tell them Jesus is risen. Two disciples walk to Emmaus with a stranger who turns out to be Jesus — they recognise Him when He breaks bread, and He vanishes. Jesus appears to all the disciples, eats fish with them, opens their minds to understand the Scriptures, promises the Holy Spirit, and leads them out to Bethany where He ascends to heaven. They return to Jerusalem with great joy.
main_lesson: Jesus is alive! He rose from the dead just as He promised, and He walks with us even when we don't recognise Him.

memory_verse.kjv: He is not here, but is risen.
memory_verse.little_bible: The angels said: 'He is not here — Jesus has risen from the dead, just like He told you He would!'

parent_guide: Luke 24 is the great resurrection chapter of Luke's Gospel and one of the most joyful passages in all of Scripture. The empty tomb (vv1-12) is followed by the incomparable Road to Emmaus story (vv13-35) — a unique account of Jesus walking unrecognised alongside grieving disciples, opening the Scriptures to them, and being revealed in the breaking of bread. The appearance to all the disciples (vv36-43) emphasises the bodily reality of the resurrection — Jesus is not a ghost; He eats fish. The opening of minds to understand Scripture (vv44-49) and the commission to preach repentance to all nations sets up Acts. The Ascension (vv50-53) closes Luke with the disciples full of joy. Key pastoral note for children: the Road to Emmaus is perfect for illustrating that Jesus walks with us in our confusion and sadness, and reveals Himself through Word and bread (worship). The disciples' hearts burned — that is what Scripture does when Jesus opens it to us.
application_for_children: Jesus is alive and He is with you right now, even when you can't see Him. When you feel sad or confused, remember — He walks beside you. He knows your name. And one day you will see Him face to face.

verses.kjv: Now upon the first day of the week, very early in the morning, they came unto the sepulchre, bringing the spices which they had prepared, and certain others with them.
verses.little_bible: Very early on Sunday morning, the women who loved Jesus got up before the sun was fully risen. They carried sweet spices to the tomb to take care of Jesus' body.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Early Sunday morning, women went to Jesus' tomb with sweet spices.
verses.meaning: These women showed up at the darkest moment with love and devotion. They didn't know what was coming — but God had prepared something they couldn't imagine.
verses.memory_phrase: Love shows up early.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me love You the way these women did — showing up for You even when things seem hard.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the women came so early in the morning?
verses.family_discussion: The women were heartbroken but they still came. What does that tell us about what love looks like even when we're sad?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind for someone today, even if you don't feel like it — love acts even when it's hard.
verses.illustration_prompt: Three women in long robes walking in the soft purple light of early dawn, carrying clay jars of spices, a rocky garden path leading to a large stone tomb ahead. Gentle mist in the air, storybook style.

verses.kjv: And they found the stone rolled away from the sepulchre.
verses.little_bible: When they arrived at the tomb, they stopped and stared. The huge stone that had been blocking the entrance — it had been rolled away!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They looked and saw the big stone had been rolled away!
verses.meaning: That stone was there to keep things in — but nothing can keep Jesus in. God had rolled it away not to let Jesus out, but so people could look in and believe.
verses.memory_phrase: Nothing can hold Jesus back.
verses.prayer: God, You move the things that seem impossible to move. Help me trust You with the big things in my life.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think the women felt when they saw the stone rolled away?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes we face things that look like closed doors. What does the rolled-away stone tell us about what God can do?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one thing that feels too hard or too big for you. Write it down or tell God about it, and remember He rolls stones away.
verses.illustration_prompt: A large round stone rolled back from a tomb entrance carved in rock, warm golden sunrise light pouring in from inside the empty tomb. Three women standing at a distance looking in shock and wonder. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And they entered in, and found not the body of the Lord Jesus.
verses.little_bible: The women stepped inside the tomb — and Jesus was not there. The place where His body had been laid was empty. He was gone.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They went inside. Jesus was not there. The tomb was empty!
verses.meaning: An empty tomb is the most important fact in human history. Death had Jesus — and then it didn't. Everything changed in that moment.
verses.memory_phrase: The tomb was empty!
verses.prayer: Thank You, Jesus, that death could not hold You. Because You are alive, I can have life too.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think you would feel if you went somewhere expecting to find something important and it was gone?
verses.family_discussion: The empty tomb changed everything for these women and for us. What does an empty tomb mean for people who love Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone today: 'Jesus is alive!' — because it is the most important truth in the world.
verses.illustration_prompt: Interior of a stone tomb, a flat rock shelf where a body would lie, folded burial cloths left behind, the room lit by golden light streaming from the open entrance. No body — just light and stillness. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as they were much perplexed thereabout, behold, two men stood by them in shining garments:
verses.little_bible: The women were completely confused, wondering what had happened — and then, suddenly, two men appeared beside them, wearing clothes that blazed and shone like lightning!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The women were confused. Then two men in shining, bright clothes appeared!
verses.meaning: These were angels — God's messengers sent at the most important moment. When we are most confused, God sends a word.
verses.memory_phrase: God speaks in our confusion.
verses.prayer: God, when I am confused and don't understand, help me look for Your message. Speak to me through Your Word.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever felt confused and then someone said something that helped? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: God sent angels to answer the women's confusion right away. What does that tell us about how God feels about our questions?
verses.do_it_today: If you have a question about God or the Bible, ask a parent or look it up — God wants you to understand.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two tall figures in dazzling white robes standing in the tomb, their garments glowing brightly, light radiating from them. Three women standing back, shielding their eyes in awe. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And as they were afraid, and bowed down their faces to the earth, they said unto them, Why seek ye the living among the dead?
verses.little_bible: The women were so frightened they bowed their faces to the ground. Then the angels said: 'Why are you looking for someone who is alive in a place for the dead?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The women bowed down, afraid. The angels said, 'Why look for the living here?'
verses.meaning: The angels' question is one of the most beautiful in Scripture — it gently corrects the women: Jesus doesn't belong among the dead. He is the Living One.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is the Living One!
verses.prayer: Lord Jesus, You are alive! Help me stop looking for You in the wrong places and find You where You truly are.
verses.discussion_question: Why would it be strange to look for a living person in a tomb?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes we act as if Jesus is far away or silent. How does the angels' question remind us to think about Jesus differently?
verses.do_it_today: Spend two minutes today just talking to Jesus like He is alive and right there with you — because He is.
verses.illustration_prompt: Women bowing low on the ground in reverence and fear, two luminous angel figures above them with hands gently extended, speaking. Light fills the space. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: He is not here, but is risen: remember how he spake unto you when he was yet in Galilee,
verses.little_bible: The angels said: 'He is not here — He has risen from the dead! Don't you remember what He told you when you were all back in Galilee?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The angels said, 'He is not here — Jesus has risen! He told you this would happen!'
verses.meaning: These are the six most world-changing words: 'He is not here, but is risen.' Jesus' resurrection is not a surprise — He predicted it. His word always comes true.
verses.memory_phrase: He is not here — He is risen!
verses.prayer: Thank You, Jesus, that You are alive! Your words always come true. Help me trust everything You say.
verses.discussion_question: If Jesus said something would happen and it did happen, should we believe the other things He said too?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus told His disciples ahead of time that He would rise. How does this make you feel about the promises Jesus has made to us?
verses.do_it_today: Find one promise Jesus made in the Bible and write it down. Remember: if He rose, He keeps His promises.
verses.illustration_prompt: A glowing angel speaking in the empty tomb, mouth open, one hand raised, a radiant expression of joy, while the burial cloths lie folded nearby. Warm gold light flooding the scene. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Saying, The Son of man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and the third day rise again.
verses.little_bible: The angels reminded them of Jesus' own words: 'He said the Son of Man would be handed over to sinful people, crucified — and on the third day, rise again!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said He would die and rise on the third day. It happened just like He said!
verses.meaning: Jesus did not stumble into death — He walked into it knowingly and walked back out of it victoriously. Every word He said came true.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus said it — and it happened.
verses.prayer: Jesus, everything You said came true. Help me trust Your words about my life and my future too.
verses.discussion_question: If someone correctly predicted something impossible, would you trust what else they say?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus predicted His death and resurrection in detail. What does that tell us about who He is?
verses.do_it_today: Read one more thing Jesus promised in the Bible and talk with your family about what it means.
verses.illustration_prompt: A simple split illustration: on one side, a cross on a hill at dusk; on the other side, an open tomb with sunrise pouring out. A reminder that both were part of God's plan. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And they remembered his words,
verses.little_bible: And then — it came back to them. They remembered. Jesus had told them this would happen.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Then they remembered. Jesus had said this would happen!
verses.meaning: Memory and faith go together. The women had heard Jesus' words before; now those words lit up with meaning. God's Word is never wasted — it waits for the right moment.
verses.memory_phrase: Remember what Jesus said.
verses.prayer: God, help me remember Your words when I need them most. Put Your Word deep in my heart.
verses.discussion_question: Has someone ever said something to you that you didn't understand at first, but later it made sense?
verses.family_discussion: Why is it important to learn God's Word even when we don't fully understand it yet?
verses.do_it_today: Learn one Bible verse by heart today so that when you need it, you will remember.
verses.illustration_prompt: Close-up of a woman's face, eyes wide open with sudden realisation, tears on her cheeks turning to wonder, dawn light reflecting in her eyes. A memory-like soft glow in the background. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And returned from the sepulchre, and told all these things unto the eleven, and to all the rest.
verses.little_bible: The women ran from the tomb and told everything to the eleven disciples and all the others who were waiting. They couldn't keep the news to themselves!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They ran and told the disciples everything they had seen and heard!
verses.meaning: Good news cannot be held in silence. The women became the first messengers of the resurrection — sent by angels to tell what they had seen.
verses.memory_phrase: Share the good news!
verses.prayer: Lord, fill me with such joy about Jesus being alive that I can't help but tell someone.
verses.discussion_question: When is the last time you heard really good news? Did you keep it to yourself or tell someone?
verses.family_discussion: The women were the first people to carry the news of the resurrection. Why is it important that everyone — including children — shares this news?
verses.do_it_today: Tell one person today: 'Jesus rose from the dead and that means everything!'
verses.illustration_prompt: Women running joyfully along a dusty path toward a group of people in the distance, robes flowing, faces lit with excitement, early morning light and long shadows behind them. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: It was Mary Magdalene, and Joanna, and Mary the mother of James, and other women that were with them, which told these things unto the apostles.
verses.little_bible: The women who told this news were Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and others. They told the apostles what had happened.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Mary Magdalene, Joanna, and others told the apostles the good news.
verses.meaning: God chose women to be the first resurrection witnesses — a remarkable detail in a culture where women's testimony was often not valued. God values every voice.
verses.memory_phrase: God uses everyone to tell His story.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that You use all kinds of people — including me — to share Your good news.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it special that God chose women to be the very first people to announce the resurrection?
verses.family_discussion: What does it tell us about God that He chose those who were often overlooked to carry the most important message ever?
verses.do_it_today: Remember that God can use YOU — no matter your age, no matter who you are — to tell people about Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of women, faces glowing with excitement, speaking earnestly to a group of men who look bewildered and astonished. Morning light, a Jerusalem street in the background. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And their words seemed to them as idle tales, and they believed them not.
verses.little_bible: But the disciples didn't believe the women. The report sounded like nonsense to them — they thought the women were just imagining things.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples didn't believe the women. It sounded too strange to be true.
verses.meaning: Even the disciples struggled to believe. Resurrection is not easy to accept. But disbelief doesn't change the truth — Jesus was still risen.
verses.memory_phrase: Truth is true even when it's hard to believe.
verses.prayer: Lord, when things are hard to believe, help me trust You anyway. Strengthen my faith.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been told something amazing that you found hard to believe at first?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples didn't believe right away — and that's okay. How does God's patience with their doubt encourage us when we have doubts too?
verses.do_it_today: If you have a doubt or question about God, be honest about it in prayer. God is not scared of your questions.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of men, faces showing scepticism and confusion, one shaking his head, another with hands raised in disbelief, as the women stand before them looking earnest. Warm-toned room, storybook style.

verses.kjv: Then arose Peter, and ran unto the sepulchre; and stooping down, he beheld the linen clothes laid by themselves, and departed, wondering in himself at that which was come to pass.
verses.little_bible: But Peter jumped up and ran to the tomb to see for himself. He bent down and looked inside — and saw the linen cloths lying there, all by themselves, with no body. He walked away wondering what it all meant.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Peter ran to the tomb. He looked in and saw the cloths — but no Jesus. He walked away amazed.
verses.meaning: Peter's reaction is so human — he had to see for himself. He found what the women reported was true. Wonder is the beginning of faith.
verses.memory_phrase: Seeing leads to wondering — wondering leads to faith.
verses.prayer: God, when I see evidence of Your truth, let it grow into wonder and then faith in my heart.
verses.discussion_question: Peter ran to see for himself. Is it okay to want evidence? What do you do when you're not sure about something?
verses.family_discussion: Peter didn't fully understand yet, but he didn't dismiss it either — he went to look. How can we model that kind of humble curiosity in our own faith?
verses.do_it_today: Look up one piece of evidence for the resurrection this week. God is not afraid of investigation.
verses.illustration_prompt: Peter bent low at the entrance of a stone tomb, peering in with wide eyes, morning sun casting long shadows behind him, folded burial cloths visible inside. A look of bewildered wonder on his face. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And, behold, two of them went that same day to a village called Emmaus, which was from Jerusalem about threescore furlongs.
verses.little_bible: That same day, two of Jesus' followers were walking away from Jerusalem toward a village called Emmaus, about seven miles away.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: That same day, two friends walked away from Jerusalem toward a village called Emmaus.
verses.meaning: They were walking away — away from Jerusalem, away from the news. Sometimes in our grief, we walk in the wrong direction. But Jesus comes and walks with us anyway.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus comes to us on the road.
verses.prayer: Jesus, come and walk with me even when I'm walking in the wrong direction. Find me the way You found them.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever walked away from something because you were sad or disappointed? What helped you?
verses.family_discussion: These two disciples were walking away from the resurrection news. How does it feel to know Jesus followed them rather than waiting for them to return?
verses.do_it_today: If you've been avoiding God or drifting, take a step back toward Him today. He is already walking toward you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two figures walking along a dusty road toward a distant village on a hillside, the late afternoon sun behind them casting long shadows, heads bowed in sadness. Rolling hills and olive trees. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And they talked together of all these things which had happened.
verses.little_bible: As they walked, they talked about everything that had happened — the arrest, the crucifixion, the empty tomb, the women's story. Their hearts were heavy.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: As they walked, they talked about everything that had happened to Jesus.
verses.meaning: Talking through our grief and confusion is healthy. These two were processing tragedy. Jesus drew near while they were in the middle of their conversation.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus draws near when we talk about Him.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me talk about You often — with my family, with friends. Be near us in those conversations.
verses.discussion_question: Is it good to talk about sad or confusing things with someone you trust? Why?
verses.family_discussion: When was the last time your family talked about something from the Bible together? How did it help you?
verses.do_it_today: Have a conversation with someone today about something God has been doing or something you are wondering about.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two figures walking side by side on a dusty path, gesturing with their hands as they speak earnestly to each other, heads bent close together. Late afternoon warm light. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that, while they communed together and reasoned, Jesus himself drew near, and went with them.
verses.little_bible: While they were deep in conversation and trying to make sense of it all — Jesus Himself quietly came up beside them and began walking with them. He was right there!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: While they talked, Jesus came and walked beside them — right there with them!
verses.meaning: This is one of the most tender moments in all of Scripture: Jesus draws near to hurting people and walks with them in their confusion. He does the same for us.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus walks with us in our questions.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You come close to me when I'm sad or confused. Walk with me today.
verses.discussion_question: How does it feel to know that Jesus wants to walk beside you even in your hardest days?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus didn't wait for them to figure everything out — He came to them in the middle of their confusion. How is that a picture of grace?
verses.do_it_today: Picture Jesus walking beside you today. Talk to Him as you walk, drive, or go through your day.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two figures on a road, and now a third figure has appeared walking beside them — the stranger slightly taller, a warm glow about Him, the disciples not yet realising who He is. Golden sunset light. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: But their eyes were holden that they should not know him.
verses.little_bible: But something was preventing them from recognising who He was. They looked right at Jesus — and didn't know it was Him. Their eyes were somehow kept from seeing the truth.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: But they could not recognise Him. They didn't know it was Jesus walking with them!
verses.meaning: God sometimes keeps us from seeing the full picture so that a greater revelation can come. Their recognition at the right moment would be unforgettable.
verses.memory_phrase: God reveals Himself at just the right time.
verses.prayer: Lord, open my eyes to see You — in the Bible, in people around me, in each day You give me.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever not recognised someone at first and then suddenly realised who they were? What was that like?
verses.family_discussion: Why do you think God sometimes hides things from us temporarily before showing us something wonderful?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to open your eyes to see Him today — in creation, in His Word, in the kindness of others.
verses.illustration_prompt: Close-up of two faces looking at the stranger between them — curious, puzzled, not yet recognising Him. The stranger's face is warm but just out of full view, slightly in shadow. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, What manner of communications are these that ye have one to another, as ye walk, and are sad?
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked them: 'What is it you two are talking about as you walk along? You look so sad.' And they stopped, their faces full of grief.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked them, 'What are you talking about? You look so sad.' They stopped.
verses.meaning: Jesus asks the question even though He knows the answer. He invites them to share their pain — because bringing our grief to Him is the beginning of healing.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus asks about our sadness.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for asking about how I feel. Help me tell You honestly when I am sad or confused.
verses.discussion_question: When someone asks you 'Are you okay?' and you're not, what do you do? Do you tell them the truth?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus already knew what had happened, but He still asked. What does that tell us about how God treats our feelings?
verses.do_it_today: Have an honest conversation with God today. Tell Him exactly what is on your heart — He can handle it.
verses.illustration_prompt: The stranger turning to the two disciples on the road with a kind, open expression, their faces showing sadness and surprise. One disciple has stopped walking and turned to face Him. Warm dusty road at sunset. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And the one of them, whose name was Cleopas, answering said unto him, Art thou only a stranger in Jerusalem, and hast not known the things which are come to pass there in these days?
verses.little_bible: One of them, named Cleopas, answered: 'Are you the only person in Jerusalem who doesn't know what has been happening these past few days?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Cleopas said, 'Don't you know what happened in Jerusalem these past few days?'
verses.meaning: The irony is beautiful and tender: Cleopas asks if the stranger hasn't heard the news — when the stranger IS the news. Jesus is never outside the story; He is the story.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is always at the centre of the story.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me realise that You are not a stranger to my situation — You are right in the middle of it.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever told someone about something important, not knowing they were the most important person in that story?
verses.family_discussion: What is the beautiful irony in Cleopas asking the risen Jesus if He hadn't heard about Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Remember today that Jesus is not on the outside of your life looking in — He is right in the middle of everything.
verses.illustration_prompt: Cleopas gesturing expressively as he speaks to the stranger, looking both grieved and slightly exasperated, while the stranger listens with a gentle, knowing smile. Dusty road, late golden light. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, What things? And they said unto him, Concerning Jesus of Nazareth, which was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people:
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'What things?' So they told Him: 'About Jesus of Nazareth — a prophet with mighty words and powerful deeds, loved by God and the people alike.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'What things?' They said, 'About Jesus of Nazareth — a powerful prophet!'
verses.meaning: They describe Jesus well — mighty in word and deed — but they still see Him only as a prophet. They don't yet see the full picture. Jesus is more.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is more than a prophet.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are not just a teacher or a prophet — You are the Son of God. Help me know You fully.
verses.discussion_question: These disciples thought of Jesus as a great prophet. Who do YOU believe Jesus is?
verses.family_discussion: Many people respect Jesus as a teacher or prophet. Why is it important to believe He is also God's Son who rose from the dead?
verses.do_it_today: Finish this sentence out loud: 'I believe Jesus is...' and tell someone.
verses.illustration_prompt: The two disciples speaking passionately to the stranger, counting off reasons on their fingers, faces full of admiration mixed with grief. The village path winds behind them. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And how the chief priests and our rulers delivered him to be condemned to death, and have crucified him.
verses.little_bible: They continued: 'But the chief priests and our rulers handed Him over to be sentenced to death — and they crucified Him.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They said, 'The rulers handed Jesus over to die. They crucified Him.'
verses.meaning: They describe the crucifixion as a defeat. But what looked like defeat was the greatest victory in history — and Jesus is about to show them that.
verses.memory_phrase: What looked like defeat was victory.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me trust that even the hardest things in my life are not outside Your plan.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever watched something that seemed like a loss, but then turned into something good?
verses.family_discussion: The cross looked like the end of everything. How does the resurrection change the way we see the cross?
verses.do_it_today: Look at something hard in your life right now and ask: how might God be working something good in it?
verses.illustration_prompt: A dark hillside with three crosses silhouetted against a stormy sky, the disciples' faces turned away in grief as they recount it. Contrast of shadow and distant light. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: But we trusted that it had been he which should have redeemed Israel: and beside all this, to day is the third day since these things were done.
verses.little_bible: They said sadly: 'We had hoped He was the one who would rescue Israel. And now it's the third day since it all happened, and we just don't know what to think.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They said sadly, 'We hoped He would save us. And now it has been three days.'
verses.meaning: 'We had hoped' — two of the saddest words in any language. But their disappointed hope was about to be transformed into something far greater than they imagined.
verses.memory_phrase: Disappointed hope leads to greater joy.
verses.prayer: God, when my hope feels lost, remind me that Your plan is better than anything I imagined.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever hoped for something that didn't happen the way you expected? How did you feel?
verses.family_discussion: Their hope was real but too small. How often do we hope for something good from God when He has something even greater planned?
verses.do_it_today: Think of a hope you have right now. Give it to God and tell Him you trust His plan — even if it looks different from what you imagined.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two figures walking with slumped shoulders, faces cast down, the posture of people who have lost something precious. The third figure walks upright beside them. Warm but melancholy late-day light. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Yea, and certain women also of our company made us astonished, which were early at the sepulchre;
verses.little_bible: They went on: 'And some women in our group astonished us — they went to the tomb very early this morning.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They said, 'Some women in our group went to the tomb early this morning and amazed us!'
verses.meaning: News of the resurrection had reached them but hadn't yet reached their hearts. They are telling the risen Jesus about the risen Jesus — without knowing it.
verses.memory_phrase: The news of Jesus travels even before the heart catches up.
verses.prayer: Lord, let the truth of Your resurrection travel from my head all the way to my heart.
verses.discussion_question: Can you know something is true but still not feel it in your heart? What is that like?
verses.family_discussion: These disciples had heard the resurrection news but hadn't believed it yet. How do we help the truth of Jesus move from our head to our heart?
verses.do_it_today: Pick one truth about Jesus and say it out loud to yourself three times slowly. Let it sink in.
verses.illustration_prompt: The two disciples gesturing back toward Jerusalem as they recount the women's story to the stranger, hands expressive, faces confused but curious. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And when they found not his body, they came, saying, that they also had seen a vision of angels, which said that he was alive.
verses.little_bible: They told the stranger: 'When the women found His body was gone, they came back saying they had seen angels — angels who said Jesus was alive!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The women saw angels who said Jesus was alive! But the men weren't sure what to think.
verses.meaning: The evidence was all there — empty tomb, angels, the word of faithful women. But unbelief kept the disciples from seeing what was right before them.
verses.memory_phrase: Evidence is there — faith opens our eyes to see it.
verses.prayer: God, open my eyes and heart to see the truth of Jesus clearly. Don't let unbelief keep me from what You are showing me.
verses.discussion_question: What evidence did these disciples have that Jesus had risen? Why do you think they still weren't sure?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes we have more evidence of God's work than we realise. What evidence of God's goodness can you see in your own life?
verses.do_it_today: List three ways you have seen God at work this week — then thank Him for each one.
verses.illustration_prompt: One disciple counting things on his fingers as he recounts the women's story — the tomb, the angels, the message — while the stranger listens intently beside him. Warm golden evening light. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And certain of them which were with us went to the sepulchre, and found it even so as the women had said: but him they saw not.
verses.little_bible: They said: 'Some of the others went to the tomb too and found everything just as the women described — but they didn't see Jesus.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Others went and found the tomb empty — but they did not see Jesus.
verses.meaning: Physical evidence can take you to the edge of faith, but only the living Jesus can carry you across. They needed to encounter Him — not just find an empty tomb.
verses.memory_phrase: The tomb was empty — and Jesus is alive.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want more than empty-tomb evidence — I want to meet You personally. Make Yourself real to me.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between knowing something happened and personally meeting the person it happened to?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples found evidence but still needed to meet Jesus. How has Jesus made Himself real to your family — not just as history but as a person you know?
verses.do_it_today: Spend five minutes in prayer — not just talking, but listening for Jesus to speak to your heart.
verses.illustration_prompt: An empty stone tomb with folded burial cloths, a figure at the entrance looking in and then looking around in bewilderment. Morning light. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Then he said unto them, O fools, and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken:
verses.little_bible: Then the stranger said: 'Oh, you are so slow to believe! Why is it so hard for your hearts to trust everything the prophets of God said?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Why are you so slow to believe what God's prophets said?'
verses.meaning: Jesus' gentle rebuke is not harsh — it is loving correction. A slow heart, not a foolish mind. The prophets had already laid it all out — but unbelief had made them slow to see.
verses.memory_phrase: Open your heart to what God has already said.
verses.prayer: Lord, forgive me when I am slow to believe. Soften my heart to trust Your Word completely.
verses.discussion_question: Is it easy or hard to trust what God has said? What makes it hard sometimes?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus says their problem was a 'slow heart' — not a lack of information. Why is the heart more important than just having the right information about Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to soften your heart today — to help you believe what He says more than what you feel.
verses.illustration_prompt: The stranger turning to the two disciples with a gentle but earnest expression, hands open and gesturing as He speaks to them. Their faces show a mix of confusion and conviction. Warm dusty road. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Ought not Christ to have suffered these things, and to enter into his glory?
verses.little_bible: He said: 'Was it not necessary that the Christ — God's Chosen One — had to go through suffering first, and then enter into His glory?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Didn't God's Chosen One have to suffer first and then enter His glory?'
verses.meaning: The cross was not a detour from God's plan — it was the plan. Suffering and glory belong together in God's story. This is the pattern for the Messiah and for all who follow Him.
verses.memory_phrase: Through suffering comes glory.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me trust You when things are hard — knowing that suffering in Your plan always leads to something glorious.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a story where someone went through something hard before something wonderful happened?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus teaches that suffering and glory are connected in God's plan. How does this change the way we think about hard seasons in our lives?
verses.do_it_today: If you are going through something difficult, tell yourself: 'This is not the end of the story.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Two parallel images in one frame: a thorny crown on one side, a radiant crown of light on the other — sorrow and glory side by side. Storybook style with symbolic imagery.

verses.kjv: And beginning at Moses and all the prophets, he expounded unto them in all the scriptures the things concerning himself.
verses.little_bible: Then, starting all the way back with Moses and going through every prophet, He opened up the whole Bible for them — showing them how every part pointed to Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Starting with Moses, Jesus explained how the whole Bible was about Him!
verses.meaning: The entire Old Testament is a signpost pointing to Jesus. He is on every page — in the lamb, in the deliverer, in the suffering servant, in the promised king.
verses.memory_phrase: The whole Bible points to Jesus.
verses.prayer: Lord, show me how the whole Bible tells Your story. Help me love Your Word more every day.
verses.discussion_question: Did you know that even the very oldest parts of the Bible were already pointing to Jesus? What stories can you think of?
verses.family_discussion: When you read the Old Testament, try to ask: 'Where is Jesus in this story?' Start with Abraham and Isaac — what do you see?
verses.do_it_today: Pick one Old Testament story you know and see if you can spot something that points to Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: The stranger walking and speaking, hands moving expressively, a long road stretching behind them lined with figures from Scripture — Moses, the prophets — all gesturing toward the horizon where light breaks. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And they drew nigh unto the village, whither they went: and he made as though he would have gone further.
verses.little_bible: As they came close to the village of Emmaus, the stranger acted as though He was going to keep walking further down the road.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: As they reached the village, the stranger seemed ready to keep walking.
verses.meaning: Jesus never forces Himself on anyone. He waited to be invited in. The disciples had to choose to welcome Him — and that choice changed everything.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus waits to be invited in.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I invite You in — into my home, my day, my heart. Don't walk past me.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus waited to be invited rather than just walking in with them?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus stands at the door and knocks — He doesn't break it down. What does that tell you about how God relates to us?
verses.do_it_today: Say these words out loud: 'Jesus, I invite You into this day and into my heart. Come in.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Two travellers at a village gate, turning to the stranger who has taken a step past them as if to continue on. Their hands are outstretched, urging Him to stay. Warm lamplight from cottage windows. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: But they constrained him, saying, Abide with us: for it is toward evening, and the day is far spent. And he went in to tarry with them.
verses.little_bible: But they urged Him strongly: 'Stay with us! It's getting late and the day is nearly over.' And He went in to be with them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They said, 'Please stay with us — it is almost evening!' And He went in with them.
verses.meaning: 'Abide with us' is one of the great prayers of Scripture. And Jesus always answers it — He went in with them. He always goes in with those who ask.
verses.memory_phrase: Ask Jesus to stay with you.
verses.prayer: Abide with me, Lord Jesus. Stay close. Don't let me walk through this day without You.
verses.discussion_question: If you could ask Jesus to stay in your home, what is one thing you would want to ask Him?
verses.family_discussion: The prayer 'Abide with us' became a famous hymn. What does it mean to invite Jesus to truly stay — not just visit — your family?
verses.do_it_today: At the end of today, say: 'Lord, thank You for staying with me today. Stay with me tonight.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A small warm cottage interior, two disciples gesturing the stranger inside, the door open with evening sky and first stars visible. Candles being lit on a simple table. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as he sat at meat with them, he took bread, and blessed it, and brake it, and gave to them.
verses.little_bible: As they sat down to eat together, the stranger took the bread, gave thanks to God, broke it, and gave it to them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: At the table, the stranger took bread, said thank You to God, broke it, and gave it to them.
verses.meaning: The same hands that fed five thousand, the same gesture from the Last Supper — something in the way Jesus broke bread was unmistakable. The familiar holy act opened their eyes.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus reveals Himself in the breaking of bread.
verses.prayer: Lord, meet me at Your table. As I remember You in worship and bread, open my eyes to see You clearly.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the way Jesus broke the bread helped them recognise Him?
verses.family_discussion: There is something sacred about sharing a meal together. How can your family make mealtimes more intentional — a place to see Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: At your next meal, take a moment before eating to truly give thanks — not just habit, but real, heartfelt thanks to Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: A warm candlelit table, the stranger's hands holding a torn piece of bread, the disciples on either side leaning forward, their faces beginning to show recognition dawning. Bread crumbs on the table. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And their eyes were opened, and they knew him; and he vanished out of their sight.
verses.little_bible: And in that instant — their eyes were opened! They knew Him — it was Jesus! And just as they recognised Him, He vanished from their sight.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Their eyes opened wide — they saw it was Jesus! And then He was gone!
verses.meaning: Recognition and disappearance in the same moment — because now they didn't need to see Him physically. They had the Word in their hearts and the Spirit in them. He could be trusted without seeing.
verses.memory_phrase: We see Jesus — and then we trust Him.
verses.prayer: Lord, open my eyes to see You — and then give me faith to trust You even when I can't see You.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus disappeared right when they recognised Him?
verses.family_discussion: After the resurrection, Jesus began teaching His followers to trust Him without seeing Him physically. How do we do that today?
verses.do_it_today: Trust Jesus with one thing today that you cannot see the answer to. He is there — even when you can't see Him.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two disciples at a table, faces lit with sudden joy and astonishment, reaching out across the table — but the seat in the middle is empty, only a warm glow remaining where Jesus had been. Broken bread still on the table. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And they said one to another, Did not our heart burn within us, while he talked with us by the way, and while he opened to us the scriptures?
verses.little_bible: They looked at each other and said: 'Didn't our hearts feel like they were on fire when He talked to us on the road? When He opened the Scriptures to us?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They said to each other, 'Didn't our hearts burn when He spoke to us about the Scriptures?'
verses.meaning: A burning heart is God's signature when His Word comes alive. That warmth, that sense that the Bible is speaking directly to your soul — that is the work of the risen Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: God's Word makes our hearts burn.
verses.prayer: Lord, make my heart burn when I read Your Word. Don't let it ever be cold or dull to me.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever felt something warm or excited in your heart when you heard something about God? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: What can your family do to keep your hearts 'burning' for God's Word? What makes Bible time feel alive rather than routine?
verses.do_it_today: Read a short passage of the Bible and ask: 'Lord, make this passage feel alive to me today.' Then listen.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two excited figures sitting at the supper table, hands on their chests, faces alight with wonder and joy, looking at each other and gesturing toward the road they walked. Warm firelight inside. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And they rose up the same hour, and returned to Jerusalem, and found the eleven gathered together, and them that were with them,
verses.little_bible: That very hour — even though it was night — they got up and walked all the way back to Jerusalem! They found the eleven disciples gathered together with the others.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Right then — even though it was night — they rushed back to Jerusalem to tell the others!
verses.meaning: The Emmaus disciples had been walking away from Jerusalem. The risen Jesus turned them around. Good news does that — it reverses our direction.
verses.memory_phrase: Resurrection joy sends us back in a hurry.
verses.prayer: Lord, let the joy of knowing You are alive give me energy and boldness to tell people about You.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever had news so exciting you had to share it immediately, even if it was inconvenient?
verses.family_discussion: They had been walking seven miles away and walked seven miles back that very night. What does that tell us about what an encounter with the risen Jesus does to a person?
verses.do_it_today: Is there someone you have been waiting to tell about Jesus? Don't wait — go now, or send a message today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two figures walking briskly through a moonlit landscape toward the lights of a city in the distance, cloaks pulled around them against the night air, faces set with purpose and joy. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Saying, The Lord is risen indeed, and hath appeared to Simon.
verses.little_bible: When they arrived, the others were already saying: 'It is true! The Lord has really risen from the dead! And He appeared to Simon Peter!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples said, 'The Lord is really risen! He appeared to Simon!'
verses.meaning: Multiple independent witnesses of the resurrection, confirming the same truth. Jesus appeared to Peter privately before this group gathering — showing grace to the one who had denied Him.
verses.memory_phrase: The Lord is risen indeed!
verses.prayer: The Lord is risen indeed! Jesus, thank You for being real, for being alive, for coming to those who failed You too.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it important that many different people saw Jesus risen, not just one person?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus appeared privately to Peter, who had denied Him. What does that tell us about how Jesus treats people who have let Him down?
verses.do_it_today: Say out loud together as a family: 'The Lord is risen indeed!' Say it like you mean it — because it is true.
verses.illustration_prompt: A room full of disciples, faces lit up, some with hands raised, one speaking excitedly to two newcomers in the doorway who are bursting to share their own news. Candles glowing. Warm joyful atmosphere. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And they told what things were done in the way, and how he was known of them in breaking of bread.
verses.little_bible: Then the two from Emmaus told the whole story — what happened on the road, and how Jesus was revealed to them when He broke the bread.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The two friends told everyone what happened on the road — and how they knew Jesus by the broken bread!
verses.meaning: Every witness adds to the tapestry of resurrection evidence. The church is built on multiple, overlapping, real testimonies. Your story of Jesus matters too.
verses.memory_phrase: Your story of Jesus is worth telling.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me tell my story of how You have been real to me. Give me courage to share it.
verses.discussion_question: If you had walked with the risen Jesus, what would you have told people?
verses.family_discussion: Every family that loves Jesus has a story — of how they came to know Him, of things He has done. What is your family's story?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone your favourite moment when God felt real to you — your own version of the Emmaus story.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two excited travellers in a doorway, gesturing dramatically as they tell their story, the room of disciples leaning in to listen, candles and firelight filling the space. A piece of bread on the table at the centre. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And as they thus spake, Jesus himself stood in the midst of them, and saith unto them, Peace be unto you.
verses.little_bible: Right while they were all talking about Jesus — Jesus Himself suddenly appeared, standing right in the middle of the room! He said: 'Peace be with you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: While they talked about Jesus, He suddenly appeared right in the room! He said, 'Peace be with you!'
verses.meaning: When people gather and talk about Jesus, He shows up. His first word is 'Peace' — not 'I told you so,' not judgment — Peace. That is Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus brings peace when He arrives.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when You come into my heart, bring Your peace. Let me hear You say 'Peace be with you.'
verses.discussion_question: If Jesus suddenly appeared in your room right now, what do you think you would feel?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus' first word to the frightened disciples was 'Peace.' What does that tell us about Jesus' heart toward us even when we've been confused or doubtful?
verses.do_it_today: Say 'Peace be with you' to every person in your home today. Let it remind you of what Jesus says to us.
verses.illustration_prompt: A room full of astonished disciples, mouths open, some stumbling backwards — and Jesus standing calmly in the centre, hands raised in greeting, face full of warmth and peace. Candles still burning. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: But they were terrified and affrighted, and supposed that they had seen a spirit.
verses.little_bible: But they were terrified! They thought they were seeing a ghost — someone who had died appearing to them. Fear flooded the room.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They were terrified! They thought they were seeing a ghost!
verses.meaning: Their first reaction was fear, not faith. Fear can prevent us from receiving the real Jesus. He was about to show them He was not a ghost but truly, physically alive.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is not a ghost — He is real.
verses.prayer: Jesus, calm my fears. Help me believe that You are real, present, and not something to be afraid of.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the disciples were scared even though they had been talking about Jesus all evening?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes we know all the right things about Jesus but still feel frightened or distant. How can we move from fear to trust?
verses.do_it_today: Name one thing that makes you feel afraid. Bring it to Jesus today and ask Him to replace that fear with peace.
verses.illustration_prompt: Disciples backed against the walls of a room, some huddled together, faces white with fear and eyes wide, while Jesus stands calmly in the doorway before them. Flickering candlelight. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Why are ye troubled? and why do thoughts arise in your hearts?
verses.little_bible: Jesus said to them gently: 'Why are you so troubled? Why do doubts rise up in your hearts?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked kindly, 'Why are you scared? Why do you doubt?'
verses.meaning: Jesus addresses their trouble and their inner doubts. He never judges His frightened disciples — He asks questions that draw them closer to truth.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is gentle with our doubts.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are patient with me when I doubt. Help me bring my doubts to You rather than letting them separate me from You.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus asked them questions rather than getting upset with their fear?
verses.family_discussion: Doubts often come from troubled hearts rather than rebellious minds. How does your family create a safe space to bring doubts to God?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one doubt you have been carrying and bring it honestly to Jesus in prayer.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with a gentle, questioning expression, hands open toward the fearful disciples, His posture patient and welcoming. The disciples beginning to soften, still afraid but listening. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Behold my hands and my feet, that it is I myself: handle me, and see; for a spirit hath not flesh and bones, as ye see me have.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Look at My hands and My feet! Touch Me and see! I am really here — a ghost doesn't have flesh and bones like you can see I have.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Look at my hands and feet! Touch me! I am really here — not a ghost!'
verses.meaning: The resurrection is physical and real. Jesus invites them to test it with their own hands. Faith in Jesus is not blind — He provides evidence for those who need it.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus says: 'Touch and see — I am real.'
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You are real — truly alive, truly here. Help my faith to be as solid as Your resurrection body.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus invited them to actually touch His hands and feet. Why does it matter that the resurrection was physical and real?
verses.family_discussion: Some people think Jesus only rose 'spiritually.' Why does it matter that His actual body rose from the dead?
verses.do_it_today: Hold your own hands and remember: the same Jesus who has nail-scarred hands is alive and holds your life in those very hands.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus extending His hands toward the disciples, the nail marks clearly visible. Disciples reaching forward tentatively with wonder on their faces. Warm golden light in the room. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And when he had thus spoken, he shewed them his hands and his feet.
verses.little_bible: As He spoke those words, He showed them His hands and His feet — the marks still there, proof of what He had been through and proof that it was truly Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Then Jesus showed them His hands and His feet with the marks from the cross.
verses.meaning: The risen Jesus still bears the marks of the cross. He didn't erase them — they are part of His eternal identity. The wounds are the evidence of His love.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus' wounds are proof of His love.
verses.prayer: Lord Jesus, Your scars are for me. You carry them forever as a reminder of how much You love me. Thank You.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus still has the marks from the nails in His hands and feet even after rising from the dead?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus kept His scars. What does it mean to you that the risen, glorified Jesus still shows what He suffered for us?
verses.do_it_today: Look at a cross today — a picture, a symbol — and think: 'Jesus' hands still bear those marks for me.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Close-up of Jesus' hands, gently turned palms-up to show nail marks, surrounded by the wondering faces of disciples peering in to look. Warm light, intimate moment. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And while they yet believed not for joy, and wondered, he said unto them, Have ye here any meat?
verses.little_bible: They were so overwhelmed with joy and wonder that they still couldn't quite believe it was real! Then Jesus said very simply: 'Do you have anything here to eat?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They were so full of joy they could barely believe it! Then Jesus said, 'Do you have any food?'
verses.meaning: Disbelief from joy is a tender thing — they were too happy to trust it was true. And then Jesus asks for food: the most practical, un-ghost-like request possible. He was really there.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is too good to be true — but He is true.
verses.prayer: Lord, when the joy of knowing You seems almost too wonderful to believe, help me trust that it is completely real.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been so surprised by something good that you had trouble believing it was real?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus asking for food is almost funny — and it is also brilliant. He proved He was really alive in the most ordinary way. How does Jesus meet us in ordinary moments?
verses.do_it_today: At your next meal, remember: Jesus ate food after rising from the dead. He is real, present, and meets us in the everyday.
verses.illustration_prompt: A room of disciples with tears of joy and wonder on their faces, some laughing and crying at the same time, while Jesus looks at them warmly and gestures toward the table. Firelight and candles glowing. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And they gave him a piece of a broiled fish, and of an honeycomb.
verses.little_bible: They gave Him a piece of cooked fish and some honeycomb — ordinary food, the kind they ate every day.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They gave Him fish and honeycomb to eat.
verses.meaning: Honey and fish — simple and real. The risen King of the universe ate their ordinary supper with them. He is not too glorious for the ordinary things of life.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus shares in our everyday life.
verses.prayer: Jesus, come and be part of my ordinary days — meals, homework, play. I want You in every part of my life.
verses.discussion_question: If Jesus came to your home for dinner, what would you serve Him?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus sharing a simple meal shows that He is not too exalted to join us in our everyday life. How can your family make space for Jesus in ordinary daily moments?
verses.do_it_today: Share a meal with someone today and say a genuine prayer of thanks, remembering that Jesus knows what it is to sit at a table with people He loves.
verses.illustration_prompt: A plate with a piece of grilled fish and golden honeycomb, a simple table in a lantern-lit room, a hand reaching for it. Warm and homely. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And he took it, and did eat before them.
verses.little_bible: And He took it — and He ate it, right there in front of them all. Real food. Real Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus took the food and ate it right in front of them! He was real!
verses.meaning: No ghost eats food. The resurrection is real, physical, and unmistakable. Jesus proved it the simplest way: He had supper.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is alive and real.
verses.prayer: Lord, Your resurrection is real. Help my faith be as solid as that: real, certain, and life-changing.
verses.discussion_question: Why was it important for the disciples to see Jesus actually eat the food?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples needed physical, undeniable proof. God gave it to them. How does God give us evidence for faith in ways suited to what we need?
verses.do_it_today: Every time you eat today, remember: the risen Jesus once ate too — and He is just as real now as He was then.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus sitting at a table among the disciples, eating calmly while they watch in wonder around Him, some leaning on each other, smiling through tears. Warm lantern light. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, These are the words which I spake unto you, while I was yet with you, that all things must be fulfilled, which were written in the law of Moses, and in the prophets, and in the psalms, concerning me.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said: 'Remember what I told you when I was with you before — everything written about Me in the law of Moses, the prophets, and the Psalms had to be fulfilled.' And it was all fulfilled.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Everything written about Me in God's Word had to come true — and it did!'
verses.meaning: Jesus references three sections of the Hebrew Bible — the whole Old Testament — pointing to Himself as its fulfilment. Every Scripture converges on Him.
verses.memory_phrase: Every word of God came true in Jesus.
verses.prayer: Lord, Your Word is true. Everything You have said and promised will come true. Help me build my life on Your words.
verses.discussion_question: If someone predicted things hundreds of years before they happened and they all came true, what would that tell you about that person?
verses.family_discussion: The Old Testament was written over many centuries by many authors — and it all pointed to Jesus. What does that tell us about who is behind the Bible?
verses.do_it_today: Read one Psalm together as a family and ask: 'How does this point to Jesus?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing before His disciples, gesturing toward a large scroll unfurled in the air around Him, with Moses and the prophets as figures on either side. Light radiating from the open scroll. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Then opened he their understanding, that they might understand the scriptures,
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus did something remarkable: He opened their minds so they could understand the Scriptures — as if a key turned and the whole Bible suddenly made sense.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Then Jesus opened their minds so they could understand the Bible!
verses.meaning: Understanding Scripture is not just an intellectual act — it is a gift Jesus gives. He opens minds as well as hearts. We need Him to understand His own Word.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus opens our minds to understand the Bible.
verses.prayer: Jesus, open my mind every time I read Your Word. Help me understand what You are saying to me.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been reading the Bible and suddenly understood something in a new way? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: We need the Holy Spirit to truly understand Scripture. How does your family pray before reading the Bible — asking Jesus to open your understanding?
verses.do_it_today: Before you read the Bible next time, say: 'Jesus, open my mind. Help me understand.' Then read and see.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus touching the forehead of one disciple gently, a warm golden glow spreading from His hand, the disciple's face lighting up with understanding. Others watching with eager faces. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And said unto them, Thus it is written, and thus it behoved Christ to suffer, and to rise from the dead the third day:
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'This is what was written: the Christ had to suffer, and then rise from the dead on the third day.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'God's Word said I had to suffer and rise from the dead on the third day.'
verses.meaning: The resurrection was not accidental or unexpected — it was scripted by God before the world began and written in Scripture long before it happened.
verses.memory_phrase: God planned the resurrection from the beginning.
verses.prayer: Lord, thank You that nothing in Your plan was an accident. You knew what You were doing. Help me trust Your plan for my life.
verses.discussion_question: How does it feel to know the resurrection was part of God's plan all along?
verses.family_discussion: God wrote the script of salvation long before Jesus came. How does this give you confidence in God's plans for your own life?
verses.do_it_today: Remember today: your life is not an accident. God has a plan for you — and He keeps every promise.
verses.illustration_prompt: An ancient scroll open in Jesus' hands, letters of gold glowing on the page, while the disciples crowd close to read. The words seem to come alive and glow. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And that repentance and remission of sins should be preached in his name among all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'And now this message must go out everywhere — forgiveness of sins through repentance in My name, starting from Jerusalem and spreading to every nation.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Tell every nation: turn to God and be forgiven — starting from Jerusalem!'
verses.meaning: The resurrection is not just for the disciples' hearts — it is news for the whole world. Every nation, every person needs to hear: forgiveness is available through Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus' good news is for the whole world.
verses.prayer: Lord, help Your good news go everywhere. Let it reach people near me and people far away who need to hear.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus said the message should go to ALL nations, not just some?
verses.family_discussion: The gospel started in Jerusalem and spread. How did it reach your family? Who told whom? Trace the path of the gospel to your home.
verses.do_it_today: Pray for one country you know of today that needs to hear about Jesus. Ask God to send His message there.
verses.illustration_prompt: A map-like illustration of the world expanding outward from a glowing Jerusalem, lines of light spreading to distant lands, people of all nations at the edges receiving the light. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And ye are witnesses of these things.
verses.little_bible: Jesus looked at them and said simply: 'You are witnesses of everything that has happened.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'You have seen all these things — you are My witnesses!'
verses.meaning: A witness is someone who tells what they have seen and heard. The disciples saw it all — and now they had a calling. So do we: we tell what Jesus has done in us.
verses.memory_phrase: You are a witness for Jesus.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me be a faithful witness — telling people what I know to be true about You.
verses.discussion_question: A witness tells what they have seen. What have you seen or experienced of God that you could tell someone?
verses.family_discussion: What has your family seen God do? What testimony do you carry that could encourage someone else?
verses.do_it_today: Tell one person today something you know is true about Jesus — from the Bible or from your own experience.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with arm extended toward the group of disciples, all looking back at Him attentively, faces serious and commissioned. Light streaming from behind Jesus. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And, behold, I send the promise of my Father upon you: but tarry ye in the city of Jerusalem, until ye be endued with power from on high.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I am going to send you the gift My Father promised — but wait in Jerusalem until you receive power from heaven. Stay until that power comes to you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Wait in Jerusalem for the gift of power from heaven that My Father promised!'
verses.meaning: The disciples could not carry out the mission on their own strength. They needed the Holy Spirit. Jesus tells them to wait — power is coming. That power is still available today.
verses.memory_phrase: Wait for God's power — then go.
verses.prayer: Holy Spirit, come and fill me with Your power. I cannot do what Jesus asks on my own — I need You.
verses.discussion_question: Why would it be important to wait for God's power before trying to tell people about Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus promised power from on high — that's the Holy Spirit. How does your family depend on the Holy Spirit rather than just trying harder?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for the Holy Spirit's power in your life today. Ask to be filled again.
verses.illustration_prompt: Disciples standing together in a circle, heads bowed in anticipation and prayer, a soft light beginning to descend from above — the feeling of something powerful and wonderful about to arrive. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And he led them out as far as to Bethany, and he lifted up his hands, and blessed them.
verses.little_bible: Jesus led His disciples out of the city all the way to Bethany. Then He lifted His hands — the nail-scarred hands — and He blessed them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus led them to Bethany. He lifted His hands and blessed them.
verses.meaning: Jesus' last act on earth before the Ascension was blessing His disciples. He left them not with a task or a warning — but with a blessing. That is how He sends us.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus sends us out with His blessing.
verses.prayer: Lord Jesus, thank You that You bless me as You send me. I go out today with Your blessing on my life.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to be blessed by someone? How does it feel to know Jesus blesses you?
verses.family_discussion: Fathers and mothers can bless their children, just as Jesus blessed His disciples. Do you have a tradition of blessing your children? This might be a good time to start.
verses.do_it_today: Ask a parent to bless you, or if you are a parent, bless your children today by speaking good words over them.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing on a hillside near Bethany, robes flowing, both hands raised high over a group of kneeling disciples below Him, a look of deep blessing and love on His face. Evening light, the city in the background. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, while he blessed them, he was parted from them, and carried up into heaven.
verses.little_bible: While He was blessing them — right in that holy moment — He began to rise up from the earth and was carried up into heaven before their eyes.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: While He blessed them, Jesus rose up into heaven before their eyes!
verses.meaning: The Ascension is Jesus' triumphant return to the Father — not an escape, but an enthronement. He goes to prepare a place, to rule, and to send the Spirit.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus rose to heaven — He rules from there.
verses.prayer: Lord Jesus, You are in heaven now, ruling over everything. I trust that You hold my life in Your hands from there.
verses.discussion_question: What do you imagine it looked like when Jesus rose up into heaven?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus is in heaven right now — not asleep, but ruling. How does knowing that Jesus is active and reigning in heaven change how we face our days?
verses.do_it_today: Look up at the sky today and remember: Jesus is alive, in heaven, and thinking of you right now.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus rising upward into a sky filled with golden clouds, robes flowing, hands still raised in blessing, the disciples below gazing upward in awe, some with arms raised toward Him. Warm and glorious light. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And they worshipped him, and returned to Jerusalem with great joy:
verses.little_bible: The disciples bowed down and worshipped Him. Then they got up and walked back to Jerusalem — and they were filled with great joy. Everything had changed.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples worshipped Jesus and walked back to Jerusalem full of great joy!
verses.meaning: They began this story walking away from Jerusalem in grief. They ended it walking back to Jerusalem in worship and joy. The risen Jesus transforms everything.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus turns our grief into joy.
verses.prayer: Jesus, fill me with the same joy these disciples had — the joy of knowing You are risen, ascended, and real.
verses.discussion_question: What changed for the disciples between the beginning of Luke 24 and the end?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples had gone from fear and grief to worship and great joy. Has your family experienced God turning sorrow into joy? Share a story.
verses.do_it_today: Worship God today — sing a song, dance, shout, or sit quietly in thankfulness. Let joy come out in some way.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of disciples walking joyfully back toward Jerusalem, some with arms raised, some laughing, faces upturned and radiant. The city visible on the hill ahead, morning light. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And were continually in the temple, praising and blessing God. Amen.
verses.little_bible: And from that day on, they were always at the temple, praising God and blessing His name. Amen — it is so, and it is wonderful.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Every day they were at the temple praising and thanking God. Amen!
verses.meaning: Luke's Gospel ends where it began — in the temple, with praise. The resurrection creates a community of worshippers. That community is still gathered today.
verses.memory_phrase: Praise God — He is worthy, every day.
verses.prayer: God, make me someone who praises You every day, not just on special days. You are worthy of all my worship, always.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to praise God? What are some ways you can praise Him?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples didn't just celebrate once — they continued daily. How can your family build a habit of daily praise?
verses.do_it_today: End today with praise. Name five things about God or things He has done that you are thankful for, out loud.
verses.illustration_prompt: The disciples gathered in a beautiful golden temple, arms raised, faces lifted in joy and praise, light streaming through high columns. A community of joyful worshippers. Storybook style.
